page: Microsoft Azure E48_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Ev5 server family. Powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark results demonstrate strong memory performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 252.4 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 126.65 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Passmark memory and database operations scores are also strong, reflecting high efficiency for memory-bound workloads. CPU-bound tasks, such as stress-ng multi-core performance and Geekbench multi-core compiling, fall within the average tier. However, large-scale LLM text generation using a 70B model is a notable bottleneck, performing poorly at 2.4 tokens per second due to the lack of GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E48_v5 offers a high density of RAM relative to compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive applications that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage. The absence of local disks means storage costs and performance depend entirely on attached remote volumes. This server is highly suited for relational database management systems, in-memory caching layers, enterprise application servers, and data processing tasks that fit within its 384.0 GB memory footprint.
description: The Microsoft Azure E48_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs across 24 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores clocked at 2.8 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate strong memory performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth at 252.4 GB/sec and strong database operation metrics. Conversely, compute-heavy tasks and large LLM text generation perform in the average to poor ranges. Qualitatively, the E48_v5 offers high memory density for memory-bound workloads, making it well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caches, and data processing applications that rely on remote storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48_v5 is an Ev5-family virtual machine with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations without local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48_v5 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for databases and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density and strong memory bandwidth for database and enterprise applications requiring remote storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
384.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
Strong memory bandwidth and database operation benchmark scores
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for relational databases, caching, and memory-bound workloads
